meta.title: Unit 3 — Pronouns & I/me (Test 5)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Identify pronouns in sentences and practice correct usage.

questions.question: What is a pronoun?
options: A word that describes a noun
A word that replaces a noun
A word that names a specific place
questions.explanation: A pronoun replaces a noun to avoid repeating the same noun in a sentence.

questions.question: "Me" can be used at the beginning of a sentence as the subject.
questions.explanation: "Me" is an object pronoun — it cannot be used as the subject. Use "I" at the start.

questions.question: The grass is tall, but ___ is soft.
questions.explanation: "the grass" is a thing — we replace it with "it".

questions.question: "We" includes the speaker and other people.
questions.explanation: Yes! "we" = the speaker + at least one other person.

questions.question: Which pronoun correctly fills this blank? ___ love dogs and cats.
questions.explanation: "I love" — 'I' is the subject before the verb.

questions.question: Tom likes basketball. → ___ likes basketball. (one-word pronoun)
questions.explanation: Tom is male — we use "He" to replace Tom.

questions.question: They are in the classroom. What noun could 'They' replace?
questions.explanation: "They" replaces a plural noun. "The students" is plural — it could become "They".

questions.question: Sort these: does the blank need 'I' or 'me'?
questions.explanation: 'I' = subject (before verb). 'me' = object (after verb). Note: '___ gave... to ___' uses I then me.

questions.question: In the sentence "Please give me the book," the pronoun 'me' is used correctly.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'me' comes after the verb 'give' (as the object). This is correct.

questions.question: Lisa and Amy are best friends. → ___ are best friends. (one-word pronoun)
questions.explanation: Lisa and Amy = two people → use "They".

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "They are in the classroom." 'They' is the subject pronoun.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'me' correctly?
options: My mom calls me every day.
questions.explanation: "My mom calls me" — 'me' is the object after the verb 'calls'. This is correct.

questions.question: In the sentence "Spotty is my dog. It likes to play fetch," the pronoun 'It' correctly replaces 'Spotty'.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'It' is used to replace an animal when gender isn't specified. This is correct.

questions.question: What is the pronoun in this sentence? "Andy is my friend. He is kind."
questions.explanation: "He" is the pronoun — it replaces "Andy" to avoid repeating his name.

questions.question: Fill in: ___ love dogs and cats. (I or me)
questions.explanation: "I" is the subject — it goes before the verb. "I love dogs and cats."

questions.question: Sort each pronoun: does it replace one person/thing or many?
questions.explanation: I/she/he = singular. we/they = plural.

questions.question: Which sentence has a mistake?
options: She always helps me with homework.
My mom calls me every day.
questions.explanation: "Me love" is wrong — 'Me' cannot be the subject. Correct: "I love dogs and cats."

questions.question: In the sentence "We go to the library," the pronoun 'We' includes the speaker.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'We' = the speaker + other people. The speaker is always included in 'we'.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "I love dogs and cats." 'I' is the subject pronoun.

questions.question: The bird is very small. → ___ is very small.
questions.explanation: "The bird" is an animal (not specified as male or female) → use "It".
